name: MVP — ClickUp Webhook → Discord Alert
description: Beginner-friendly HITL MVP: receive a ClickUp event, validate payload, format details, check human approval, then notify Discord or return rejected

parameters.content:
## Start Here
This workflow listens for a task event from ClickUp and only sends a Discord alert after human approval.

Flow: **ClickUp event → validate payload → format details → approval check → approved: Discord + success / rejected: return rejected**

## HITL Decision Rule
This version already includes a human approval gate.

Use input field: **human_approved**
- true = send to Discord
- false (or missing) = do not send; return rejected

nodes.name: Webhook Trigger
nodes.notes: Start point. ClickUp sends data here when a task event happens (for example: task created or updated).

nodes.name: Prepare Task Data
nodes.notes: This step cleans and organizes task details into one consistent format. It also reads human_approved (true/false) and marks whether the payload includes the minimum required fields.

nodes.name: Payload Validity Check
nodes.notes: Friendly guardrail. If required data is missing, this path returns a clear error message instead of failing later.

nodes.name: Return Invalid Payload
nodes.notes: Used when the webhook payload is incomplete. This gives a human-readable fix message.

nodes.name: Approval Check
nodes.notes: Decision step. If human_approved is true, continue to Discord. If not, stop notification and return rejected.

nodes.name: Send Discord Notification
nodes.notes: Runs only after approval. Posts a human-readable task summary to Discord so the team can react quickly.

nodes.name: Return Success
nodes.notes: Sends success only for approved requests (after Discord send).

nodes.name: Return Rejected
nodes.notes: Used when approval is false or missing. No Discord message is sent on this path.

parameters.content:
## Beginner Testing
1) Send payload with human_approved = true
2) Confirm Discord message appears and response is success
3) Send payload with human_approved = false
4) Confirm no Discord post and response is rejected
5) Send payload missing task.id or task.name
6) Confirm response is invalid_payload with a friendly fix message

If one step fails, check the previous node output first.

## Good Payload Template (Copy/Paste)
Use this shape for reliable tests:

{
  "human_approved": true,
  "task": {
    "id": "123",
    "name": "Follow up with client",
    "description": "Call at 2pm",
    "list": { "name": "Sales" },
    "status": { "status": "open" },
    "priority": { "priority": "high" },
    "assignees": [{ "username": "alex" }],
    "url": "https://app.clickup.com/t/123"
  }
}

Tip: change human_approved to false to test rejected path.
